title: Fermented Canadian Turkey Poutine
description: A twist on the classic Canadian dish featuring tender, fermented flavors and a char-grilled twist. This recipe is perfect for a quick weeknight meal or a fun, raw-friendly option for kids.

ingredients.name: Smoked Turkey Neck
ingredients.notes: Fermented in a brine solution for 24 hours before use

ingredients.name: Potatoes
ingredients.notes: Thinly sliced for a crispy poutine topping

ingredients.name: Homemade Char-Grill Sauce
ingredients.notes: Made with garlic, ginger, and apple cider vinegar

ingredients.name: All-Purpose Flour
ingredients.notes: For dusting the turkey neck before fermentation

ingredients.name: Miso Paste
ingredients.notes: Adds depth to the char-grill sauce

ingredients.name: Cheddar Cheese Curds
ingredients.notes: Fresh, high-quality cheese is a must for this recipe

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C) and set up a char-grill station with a gas grill or stovetop.
Mix together the flour, miso paste, and a pinch of salt in a small bowl for dusting the turkey neck.
Place the turkey neck on a wire rack set over a baking sheet and dust with the flour mixture, making sure to coat evenly.
Transfer the turkey neck to the char-grill station and cook for 10-12 minutes, or until nicely charred and tender.
Meanwhile, slice the potatoes into thin rounds and fry in hot oil until crispy and golden brown.
In a small saucepan, combine the char-grill sauce ingredients and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
To assemble the poutine, place a scoop of fries on a plate, top with shredded cheese curds, and spoon the char-grill sauce over the top.
Garnish with chopped chives and serve immediately.
